vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes file permissions on a backup shadow group file using a standard chmod command. This is a typical STIG hardening step with no impact on system services, SSH, or boot processes. The change is low risk and can be applied safely.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; file permission misconfiguration; potential unauthorized read of group shadow data
